source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Werner wants to label each side and each corner point of the rhombus shown with exactly one number. He wants the number on each side to be equal to the sum of the numbers on the corner points of that sides. Which number is he going to write in the place of the question mark? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'the rhombus shown' and asks for the number at a question mark placed in the figure
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual layout carries essential information
image_manifest shows an existing image asset and registry_passed=true, so the image is available for downstream use
multi_step: the rule 'number on each side equals the sum of the numbers on the corner points of that side' implies combining information from vertices and sides rather than reading off a single value
solving for the question mark in a labeled rhombus typically requires setting up and using multiple relations across the figure
the task is multimodal because the numeric relationships are defined in text but the actual labeled positions are in the image
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single number ('12'), which is easy to compare against a computed result
the stated rule provides a concrete consistency condition that can be checked against the figure
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, and asset registry passed with no recorded issues
image dependency is high, but the required image asset exists, so the core semantics are available for downstream recovery
initial scoring is strong overall, with high image_dependency_score, multi_step_score, and verifiability_score
numeric answer format makes the sample easy to verify once the visual labels are read
risk flags indicate follow-up work may be needed for visual label extraction, but they do not remove the sample's core semantic support
